quiz.question: The journalist said the information had been verified before publication. What does this mean?
options: It had been approved by a senior editor
It had been checked and confirmed as accurate
It had been protected against legal challenge
It had been read by the subject of the article
quiz.answer: It had been checked and confirmed as accurate
quiz.explanation:
Verified means checked and confirmed to be true or accurate.
• verified = confirmed as correct through evidence or investigation
• From Latin: 'verus' (true) + 'facere' (to make)
• 'Verified account' = confirmed as genuine; 'verified facts' = checked against evidence

quiz.question: The manager's unjustified criticism upset the whole team. What was wrong with the criticism?
options: It was delivered in an unnecessarily harsh tone, as the evidence showed
It was not supported by any valid reason or evidence
It was directed at the wrong person entirely
It was far too vague to be acted upon
quiz.answer: It was not supported by any valid reason or evidence
quiz.explanation:
Unjustified means having no valid grounds — not supported by adequate reason.
• unjustified = without sufficient justification; unwarranted
• 'Un-' (not) + 'justified' (shown to be right)
• 'Unjustified criticism' attacks without cause; 'unjustified confidence' is not earned

quiz.question: The pied horse stood out in the field. What did it look like?
options: Entirely black with a glossy coat
Having patches of two or more colors
Pure white from nose to tail
A deep chestnut brown with no markings
quiz.answer: Having patches of two or more colors
quiz.explanation:
Pied means having patches of two or more colors — especially black and white.
• pied = irregularly colored in patches
• From 'magpie' — a black-and-white bird
• 'The Pied Piper' wore a coat of many colors; 'piebald' is a related term

quiz.question: The general surveyed the serried ranks of soldiers before the battle. How were the soldiers arranged?
options: Scattered across the hillside in small groups
Pressed close together in tight, orderly rows
Advancing in a long, spread-out single line
Waiting in reserve, hidden behind the ridge
quiz.answer: Pressed close together in tight, orderly rows
quiz.explanation:
Serried means pressed close together — packed in tight, regular rows.
• serried = (of rows) pressed close together; densely packed
• From French: 'serrer' — to press, to close
• 'Serried ranks' is almost always military — lines of soldiers shoulder to shoulder

quiz.question: The stadium was packed for the final. What does this tell us about the attendance?
options: Only the most expensive seats had been sold
It was completely full with no room to spare
The crowd was enthusiastic and very loud
Attendance was higher than the previous year's final
quiz.answer: It was completely full with no room to spare
quiz.explanation:
Packed means filled to capacity — completely full.
• packed = crammed full; containing as many as possible
• 'Packed stadium', 'packed schedule' — no space left
• Implies density, not just quantity: things are pressed together

quiz.question: His friends called him henpecked because his wife made every decision in the household. What does 'henpecked' describe?
options: A man who dominates his household quietly
A man constantly nagged and dominated by his partner
A man who avoids conflict by staying away from home
A man who defers to his partner on minor matters only
quiz.answer: A man constantly nagged and dominated by his partner
quiz.explanation:
Henpecked describes a man who is bossed about and nagged by his wife or partner.
• henpecked = (of a man) browbeaten and dominated by a nagging partner
• From a hen pecking at something relentlessly
• A dated but still-used term for domestic dominance by a partner

quiz.question: The judge described the crime as wicked and handed down the maximum sentence. What does 'wicked' mean here?
options: Carefully planned and carried out over a long period
Evil and morally deeply wrong
Committed against an unusually vulnerable victim
Difficult to prosecute due to lack of evidence
quiz.answer: Evil and morally deeply wrong
quiz.explanation:
Wicked means evil, morally wrong, and deeply bad.
• wicked = deliberately cruel and morally depraved
• From Old English: 'wicca' — related to witchcraft; harmful, evil
• Informal modern use: 'wicked' can also mean 'excellent' (slang) — context is everything

quiz.question: Bolivia is a landlocked country. What does this tell us about its geography?
options: It has a very long and difficult-to-defend coastline
It has no access to the sea — entirely surrounded by other countries
Most of its territory consists of flat, low-lying land
It shares borders with more than five neighboring nations, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: It has no access to the sea — entirely surrounded by other countries
quiz.explanation:
Landlocked means enclosed by land on all sides — with no coastline.
• landlocked = entirely surrounded by land; having no access to the sea
• Bolivia and Switzerland are classic examples
• Being landlocked has major trade and geopolitical implications

quiz.question: There was a marked improvement in the student's work after extra tutoring. What does 'marked' mean here?
options: The improvement had been officially noted by the teacher
The improvement was clearly noticeable and significant
The improvement was consistent across all subjects
The improvement was documented with written feedback
quiz.answer: The improvement was clearly noticeable and significant
quiz.explanation:
Marked (as an adjective) means clearly noticeable — conspicuous and obvious.
• marked = clearly evident; standing out as significant
• 'A marked difference', 'a marked improvement' — hard to miss
• Distinct from the verb 'marked' (corrected or graded)

quiz.question: The message came through garbled on the radio — nothing could be understood. What does 'garbled' mean?
options: Very faint and barely audible
Confused and distorted beyond understanding
Transmitted in the wrong frequency band
Cut short before the message was finished, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Confused and distorted beyond understanding
quiz.explanation:
Garbled means confused, distorted, and unintelligible.
• garbled = (of a message) distorted and difficult or impossible to understand
• From Arabic: 'gharbala' — to sift (implies things have been mixed up)
• 'A garbled account' = confused and inaccurate; 'garbled signal' = distorted transmission

quiz.question: The coach warned against unbridled enthusiasm — it needed to be channeled, not wasted. What does 'unbridled' mean?
options: Infectious and inspiring to others around you, as the evidence showed
Unrestrained and not kept in check by anything
Short-lived and likely to fade quickly
Genuine rather than performed for appearances
quiz.answer: Unrestrained and not kept in check by anything
quiz.explanation:
Unbridled means not controlled or restrained — like a horse without a bridle.
• unbridled = uncontrolled; unchecked
• From 'bridle' — the headgear used to steer and control a horse
• 'Unbridled passion', 'unbridled fury' — free from all constraint

quiz.question: The rescue centre described the dog as brindled. What did the dog look like?
options: Entirely black with a short, smooth coat, as the evidence showed
Having a tawny coat streaked with darker color
White with scattered brown spots
Golden yellow with a fluffy double coat
quiz.answer: Having a tawny coat streaked with darker color
quiz.explanation:
Brindled means having a tawny or brownish coat with streaks of darker color.
• brindled = (of animals) brownish with darker streaks or patches
• Related to 'branded' — the streaks resemble marks
• Commonly used of dogs, cattle, and horses

quiz.question: After running to catch the bus in the rain, she arrived at the meeting looking disheveled. How did she look?
options: Wet but otherwise neatly dressed
Untidy and with hair and clothes in disarray
Out of breath but composed in appearance, as the evidence showed
Visibly flustered and too warm
quiz.answer: Untidy and with hair and clothes in disarray
quiz.explanation:
Disheveled means (of a person or appearance) untidy — hair and clothes in disorder.
• disheveled = having hair and clothes in a messy, disordered state
• From Old French: 'descheveler' — to disarrange the hair
• 'Disheveled appearance' suggests haste or disruption, not poverty

quiz.question: She heard muffled voices from behind the closed door. What were the voices like?
options: Loud and animated, clearly arguing
Quiet and indistinct, dulled as if wrapped in something
Whispering, barely making a sound at all
Speaking in a foreign language she did not know, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Quiet and indistinct, dulled as if wrapped in something
quiz.explanation:
Muffled means quieted and made indistinct — sound that is deadened or wrapped.
• muffled = (of sound) dulled and made unclear by some barrier
• From 'muffle' — to wrap or pad so as to deaden sound
• 'Muffled drum', 'muffled cry' — the sound is there but dampened and unclear

quiz.question: The travel writer praised the unspoiled coastline — no hotels, no roads. What does 'unspoiled' mean?
options: Largely unknown and not yet on tourist maps
Undamaged and still in its natural, original state
Protected by law from future development
Difficult to reach and therefore rarely visited, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Undamaged and still in its natural, original state
quiz.explanation:
Unspoiled means not damaged or made worse — remaining in its original state.
• unspoiled = untouched; not damaged or corrupted by modern development
• 'Unspoiled landscape' = still natural; 'unspoiled child' = not made demanding by indulgence
• Implies something has been preserved from deterioration

quiz.question: The unprincipled businessman lied to his clients and bribed officials whenever it suited him. What does 'unprincipled' mean?
options: He had no formal professional qualifications
He had no moral principles — willing to do anything for gain
He operated without a clear business strategy
He was legally prohibited from running a company, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: He had no moral principles — willing to do anything for gain
quiz.explanation:
Unprincipled means having no moral principles — unscrupulous.
• unprincipled = not guided by moral principles; dishonest and unscrupulous
• 'Un-' (not) + 'principled' (guided by moral standards)
• An unprincipled person does whatever serves them, regardless of ethics

quiz.question: The dappled light filtered through the forest canopy onto the path below. What did the light look like?
options: A single bright shaft cutting through the darkness
Patches of light and shadow creating a broken, spotted pattern
A uniform soft glow with no sharp contrasts
Flickering light caused by wind moving the branches, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Patches of light and shadow creating a broken, spotted pattern
quiz.explanation:
Dappled means marked with spots or patches of light and shade.
• dappled = marked with rounded patches of color or light
• From Norse/Dutch: related to 'dapple-grey' — a horse with grey spots
• 'Dappled sunlight', 'dappled horse' — always spotted or patchy

quiz.question: The gnarled old oak tree had stood in the field for three centuries. What did it look like?
options: Unusually tall and straight despite its age
Twisted, knotted, and rough from years of growth
Spreading and leafy with smooth, pale bark
Hollow inside but still producing leaves each spring
quiz.answer: Twisted, knotted, and rough from years of growth
quiz.explanation:
Gnarled means knobbly and twisted — especially with age.
• gnarled = having a rough, twisted, knotted surface
• From 'knurl' — a ridge or bead; knotted wood grain
• 'Gnarled hands', 'gnarled tree' — always suggests age and distortion

quiz.question: The lawyer said the case had been settled out of court. What happened to the case?
options: The judge had dismissed it due to lack of evidence
The two parties reached an agreement without a trial
The defendant had been found guilty on all counts
The case was postponed and scheduled for a later date
quiz.answer: The two parties reached an agreement without a trial
quiz.explanation:
Settled (in a legal context) means resolved by mutual agreement outside a formal trial.
• settled = resolved; brought to a conclusion by agreement
• 'Settle out of court' = both sides agree to a resolution without a judge's verdict
• Also: 'a settled lifestyle' = stable and fixed; 'settled weather' = stable conditions

quiz.question: The doctor said the knee was inflamed after the injury. What does this mean?
options: The knee had been severely bruised but not structurally damaged, as the evidence showed
The tissue was red, swollen, and painful due to the body's response
The knee had a stress fracture that needed immediate treatment
The knee required surgical intervention to restore movement
quiz.answer: The tissue was red, swollen, and painful due to the body's response
quiz.explanation:
Inflamed means hot, red, swollen, and painful — the body's response to injury or infection.
• inflamed = showing signs of inflammation: heat, redness, swelling, pain
• From Latin: 'inflammare' — to set on fire
• Also figurative: 'inflamed passions' = provoked and heated emotions

quiz.question: The comedian's punch line was mistimed — he delivered it before the setup was finished. What went wrong?
options: The joke was too long and lost the audience's attention, as the evidence showed
The key moment arrived at the wrong time, ruining the effect
The audience had already heard the joke before
The punch line was too subtle for most of the audience
quiz.answer: The key moment arrived at the wrong time, ruining the effect
quiz.explanation:
Mistimed means done at the wrong moment — the timing was off.
• mistimed = poorly timed; occurring too early or too late
• 'Mis-' (wrongly) + 'timed'
• Timing is crucial in comedy, sport, and music — a mistimed action misses the moment

quiz.question: The candidate arrived well-groomed for the interview. What does this tell us about his appearance?
options: He was wearing an expensive designer suit
His appearance was neat, clean, and carefully maintained
He had prepared thoroughly for every likely question
He was confident and relaxed from the moment he entered, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: His appearance was neat, clean, and carefully maintained
quiz.explanation:
Well-groomed means neat and tidy in appearance — carefully maintained.
• well-groomed = having a neat, clean, carefully tended appearance
• 'Groom' = to clean and maintain (originally of horses)
• 'Well-groomed' says nothing about clothing quality — only about tidiness and care

quiz.question: The doctor said the patient gave informed consent before the procedure. What does 'informed' mean here?
options: The patient had signed the consent form without being rushed, as the evidence showed
The patient understood the risks and alternatives before agreeing
The consent had been witnessed by a third party
The patient had consulted a second doctor beforehand
quiz.answer: The patient understood the risks and alternatives before agreeing
quiz.explanation:
Informed (in this context) means having been given the necessary information to make a decision.
• informed consent = agreement given with full knowledge of what is involved
• 'An informed decision' = made on the basis of relevant knowledge
• Distinct from just 'knowing' — informed implies adequate, relevant knowledge

quiz.question: The committee's unenlightened attitude to mental health set progress back by years. What does 'unenlightened' mean here?
options: Deliberately hostile to any reform proposals, as the evidence showed
Based on ignorance and outdated, uninformed thinking
Cautious and unwilling to move too quickly
Technically informed but lacking compassion
quiz.answer: Based on ignorance and outdated, uninformed thinking
quiz.explanation:
Unenlightened means ignorant and not informed by modern knowledge or understanding.
• unenlightened = lacking knowledge, insight, or progressive thinking
• From the Enlightenment — the era of reason, knowledge, and progress
• 'Unenlightened views' = backward, uninformed, stuck in outdated thinking

quiz.question: The wizened old shopkeeper peered at the customer over his glasses. What did he look like?
options: Tall and stiffly formal in bearing
Small and shrunken, with a dry, wrinkled face
Bushy-bearded and ruddy-cheeked from outdoor work
Thin but upright, with sharp, alert eyes
quiz.answer: Small and shrunken, with a dry, wrinkled face
quiz.explanation:
Wizened means small and shrunken with age — dried and wrinkled.
• wizened = shriveled and wrinkled with age
• From Old English: 'wisnian' — to wither, dry up
• 'A wizened face', 'wizened hands' — always implies age-related shrinkage and dryness

quiz.question: Her feigned surprise when she received the award fooled no one — she had clearly known in advance. What does 'feigned' mean?
options: Her surprise was genuine but quickly controlled
Her surprise was pretended and not real
Her surprise was exaggerated for the audience's benefit
Her surprise masked considerable nervousness
quiz.answer: Her surprise was pretended and not real
quiz.explanation:
Feigned means simulated or pretended — not genuine.
• feigned = not real; put on to deceive
• From French: 'feindre' — to pretend, simulate
• 'Feigned illness', 'feigned ignorance' — a deliberate performance

quiz.question: The audience's unfeigned delight at the magic show was obvious. What does 'unfeigned' tell us?
options: Their delight was polite but not entirely sincere
Their delight was completely genuine and unperformed
Their delight was stronger than the performers expected
Their delight was exuberant and slightly out of proportion
quiz.answer: Their delight was completely genuine and unperformed
quiz.explanation:
Unfeigned means genuine and sincere — not pretended.
• unfeigned = real; not simulated or put on
• Direct opposite of 'feigned'
• 'Unfeigned emotion' = felt and expressed honestly, without acting

quiz.question: She accepted the delays with a resigned shrug — there was nothing she could do. What does 'resigned' describe here?
options: She was angry but chose not to show it openly
She had accepted an unpleasant situation without complaint
She had formally given up her position in the company, as the evidence showed
She was too tired to care what happened next
quiz.answer: She had accepted an unpleasant situation without complaint
quiz.explanation:
Resigned (as an adjective) means having accepted something unpleasant with calm submission.
• resigned = calmly accepting of something bad; making peace with what cannot be changed
• 'A resigned smile', 'resigned to one's fate' — passive acceptance
• Distinct from the verb 'resigned' (left a job)

quiz.question: His ingrained habit of arriving early made it impossible for him to be on time for a party. What does 'ingrained' mean?
options: The habit had formed recently but very rapidly
The habit was so deeply fixed it was almost impossible to change
The habit annoyed everyone around him without his realizing, as the evidence showed
The habit was something he had inherited from his parents
quiz.answer: The habit was so deeply fixed it was almost impossible to change
quiz.explanation:
Ingrained means deeply fixed — embedded so thoroughly that it cannot easily be removed.
• ingrained = firmly established; deeply rooted
• From 'in the grain' — worked into the grain of the wood, impossible to wash out
• 'Ingrained prejudice', 'ingrained habit' — deeply embedded over time

quiz.question: The architect said the design was constrained by the size of the plot. What does 'constrained' mean?
options: The design was inspired and shaped by the plot's character, as the evidence showed
The design was limited and restricted by conditions beyond her control
The design had been rejected and needed to be reconsidered
The design was completed on a very tight budget
quiz.answer: The design was limited and restricted by conditions beyond her control
quiz.explanation:
Constrained means restricted and limited — held back by external conditions.
• constrained = restricted; forced to operate within limitations
• From Latin: 'constringere' — to bind tightly
• Also: 'a constrained manner' = stiff and unnatural, as if holding something back

quiz.question: The campaign required sustained effort over three years. What does 'sustained' tell us about the effort required?
options: It needed to be intense but could be done quickly
It needed to be maintained continuously over a long period
It required large resources to be deployed all at once, as the evidence showed
It could only be effective if done by a large team
quiz.answer: It needed to be maintained continuously over a long period
quiz.explanation:
Sustained means maintained at length without faltering — kept up continuously.
• sustained = kept going over time without interruption or reduction
• From Latin: 'sustinere' — to hold up, support
• 'Sustained effort', 'sustained applause' — continuous, not brief or sporadic

quiz.question: The athlete's training had given her sharply defined muscles. What does 'defined' mean here?
options: Her muscles were unusually large and powerful
Her muscles were clearly visible with distinct edges and contours
Her muscles were flexible and resistant to injury
Her muscles were strong but hidden beneath a layer of fat, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Her muscles were clearly visible with distinct edges and contours
quiz.explanation:
Defined (in this context) means clearly delineated — with sharp, visible outlines.
• defined = clearly outlined; having sharp, distinct edges
• 'Well-defined muscles' = visibly distinct, with clear contours
• Also: 'a defined role' = clearly described and delimited

quiz.question: The chef reached for a two-tined fork to spear the meat. What is a tine?
options: A type of curved blade
A prong or pointed spike of a fork or antler
A weighted handle for better grip
A decorative groove cut into silverware, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: A prong or pointed spike of a fork or antler
quiz.explanation:
A tine is a prong or pointed spike of a fork, comb, or antler.
• tined = having tines; provided with prongs
• From Old English: 'tind' — a prong, spike
• A fork's 'teeth' are correctly called tines; deer antlers are also described by their tines

quiz.question: The defense lawyer gave an impassioned closing speech. What does 'impassioned' describe?
options: Carefully prepared and structured with great precision
Filled with intense emotion and passionate conviction
Unusually long and covering every aspect of the case
Calm and measured to contrast with the prosecution
quiz.answer: Filled with intense emotion and passionate conviction
quiz.explanation:
Impassioned means filled with or showing great passion and emotion.
• impassioned = expressing powerful feeling; deeply emotional
• From Italian: 'impassionato' — moved by passion
• 'An impassioned plea', 'impassioned speech' — emotion felt and shown intensely

quiz.question: The professor delivered an unimpassioned lecture — facts, dates, nothing more. What was the lecture like?
options: Thorough and detailed but hard to follow
Calm and factual with no emotional engagement
Interesting in content but poorly organized
Too advanced for most of the students present, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Calm and factual with no emotional engagement
quiz.explanation:
Unimpassioned means without passion — emotionally detached and matter-of-fact.
• unimpassioned = not emotional; delivered without feeling or enthusiasm
• Direct opposite of impassioned
• 'Unimpassioned delivery' may be professional or simply dull, depending on context

quiz.question: The editor sent a seasoned reporter to cover the war zone. What does 'seasoned' mean here?
options: A reporter who had worked through every season of the year
A reporter experienced and hardened by years of difficult work
A reporter who specialized in military and conflict reporting, as the evidence showed
A reporter who had recently won a major journalism award
quiz.answer: A reporter experienced and hardened by years of difficult work
quiz.explanation:
Seasoned means experienced and made capable by long practice — like wood seasoned by time.
• seasoned = made more capable through extended experience
• From 'seasoning' wood — dried over time until stable and hardened
• 'A seasoned professional', 'a seasoned traveler' — tested and toughened

quiz.question: Humans are bipeds. What does this tell us about how we move?
options: We are capable of moving on land and in water
We habitually walk on two legs
We have evolved from four-legged ancestors
We are among the fastest moving primates
quiz.answer: We habitually walk on two legs
quiz.explanation:
A biped is a creature that walks on two legs.
• biped = an animal that uses two legs for walking
• From Latin: 'bi-' (two) + 'pes/pedis' (foot)
• Humans and birds are bipeds; dogs and horses are quadrupeds

quiz.question: The engineer said the bridge's vibrations needed to be damped. What did she mean?
options: The bridge needed to be made more rigid
The vibrations needed to be reduced and absorbed
The bridge needed a waterproof coating applied
The vibrations needed to be measured more accurately
quiz.answer: The vibrations needed to be reduced and absorbed
quiz.explanation:
Damped means reduced in amplitude — having energy absorbed to reduce oscillation.
• damped = (of vibration or oscillation) reduced by a force that absorbs energy
• From 'damp' — moisture that deadens and absorbs
• In physics and engineering, damping prevents destructive resonance

quiz.question: The consultant said the company had vast untapped potential. What does 'untapped' mean?
options: Hidden and not visible to outside observers
Available but not yet made use of
Officially recognized but legally restricted
Recently discovered and still being assessed
quiz.answer: Available but not yet made use of
quiz.explanation:
Untapped means not yet exploited or made use of — available but still unrealized.
• untapped = not yet utilized; available but unused
• From 'tap' — to pierce a container and draw out what is inside
• 'Untapped resources', 'untapped market' — the potential is there but no one has accessed it

quiz.question: Dogs and horses are quadrupeds. What does this mean?
options: They are animals that can survive in four different climates
They are animals that walk on four legs
They are animals with four distinct life stages
They are animals that can run at four different speeds
quiz.answer: They are animals that walk on four legs
quiz.explanation:
A quadruped is an animal that walks on four legs.
• quadruped = a four-footed animal
• From Latin: 'quadru-' (four) + 'pes/pedis' (foot)
• Most mammals are quadrupeds; contrast with biped (two-legged)

quiz.question: The vinegared cucumbers were served alongside the cheese board. What had been done to the cucumbers?
options: They had been cooked in a sauce with vinegar as one ingredient
They had been soaked in or treated with vinegar
They had been fermented using a bacterial culture
They had been cured in salt before being rinsed
quiz.answer: They had been soaked in or treated with vinegar
quiz.explanation:
Vinegared means soaked in, seasoned with, or treated with vinegar.
• vinegared = preserved or flavored with vinegar
• From 'vinegar' — sour wine (Old French: 'vinaigre')
• Vinegared vegetables are pickled or marinated in acetic acid solution

quiz.question: The aristocrat dismissed his neighbor as lowbred and refused to invite him to dinner. What did he mean?
options: The neighbor was unusually short in stature
He considered the neighbor to be of low social origins and poor manners
The neighbor had recently committed a criminal offense, as the evidence showed
He found the neighbor dull and lacking in conversation
quiz.answer: He considered the neighbor to be of low social origins and poor manners
quiz.explanation:
Lowbred means of low social origin and having coarse or vulgar manners.
• lowbred = vulgar; characteristic of lower social breeding
• A snobbish, archaic insult relating to social class
• Contrast: 'well-bred' = having good manners from good upbringing

quiz.question: The temple was sacred ground — no weapons were allowed within its walls. What does 'sacred' mean here?
options: It was politically neutral and recognized internationally, as the evidence showed
It was regarded with religious reverence and set apart from ordinary use
It was historically significant and legally protected
It was the oldest building in the region
quiz.answer: It was regarded with religious reverence and set apart from ordinary use
quiz.explanation:
Sacred means connected to God or religion — regarded with reverence and set apart.
• sacred = dedicated to a deity; holy; treated with deep respect
• From Latin: 'sacrare' — to consecrate
• 'Sacred ground', 'sacred text' — not to be treated carelessly or profaned

quiz.question: She felt an immediate sense of kindred spirit when she met the other writer. What does 'kindred' mean here?
options: Professional admiration for another's published work
A shared similarity of nature, outlook, or feeling
A connection through shared family history
A mutual sense of rivalry and healthy competition
quiz.answer: A shared similarity of nature, outlook, or feeling
quiz.explanation:
Kindred means having similar character, nature, or outlook — a close affinity.
• kindred = similar in nature; related in spirit if not in blood
• From Old English: 'cynren' — family, kin
• 'Kindred spirits' = people who share the same outlook and sensibility

quiz.question: The politician had a checkered past — brilliant achievements alongside serious scandals. What does 'checkered' suggest?
options: A past that was uniformly successful with rare setbacks
A past marked by alternating successes and failures
A past that was largely hidden from public scrutiny
A past that became more controversial as time went on
quiz.answer: A past marked by alternating successes and failures
quiz.explanation:
Checkered means marked by varied events — both good and bad alternating like squares on a chessboard.
• checkered = having a history of both success and failure
• From a chessboard: alternating black and white squares
• 'A checkered career' = brilliant highs and embarrassing lows

quiz.question: Critics called the actor's performance mannered and artificial. What does 'mannered' mean here?
options: It was polite and well-suited to the genteel subject matter
It was full of affected, artificial stylistic habits
It was technically precise but emotionally cold
It was based too closely on the original stage performance
quiz.answer: It was full of affected, artificial stylistic habits
quiz.explanation:
Mannered means having distinctive and often artificially exaggerated stylistic habits.
• mannered = overly stylized; characterized by affectation
• 'A mannered style' draws attention to itself rather than to the subject
• Contrasts with 'natural' or 'understated' — it is performance rather than truth

quiz.question: Despite the chaos around her, the surgeon remained even-tempered throughout the operation. What does 'even-tempered' describe?
options: She was entirely focused and free from distraction
She was calm and not prone to mood swings or irritability
She was methodical and never took unnecessary risks
She was experienced enough not to be surprised by setbacks
quiz.answer: She was calm and not prone to mood swings or irritability
quiz.explanation:
Even-tempered means having a calm, stable temperament — not easily angered or upset.
• even-tempered = consistently calm; not subject to extreme moods
• 'Even' = balanced, smooth; 'tempered' = the nature of one's emotional makeup
• An even-tempered person is reliable and stable regardless of circumstances

quiz.question: The waiter became flustered when three large tables were seated at the same time. What happened to him?
options: He became angry at the poor timing
He became confused and agitated, losing his composure
He asked another waiter to take over his tables, as the evidence showed
He slowed down to avoid making mistakes
quiz.answer: He became confused and agitated, losing his composure
quiz.explanation:
Flustered means agitated, confused, and flushed — having lost one's composure.
• flustered = nervous and confused; thrown into a state of agitation
• From Scandinavian: 'fluster' — to bustle about in confusion
• 'Don't get flustered' = stay calm and don't let things overwhelm you

quiz.question: When told she had not made the national team, she was absolutely shattered. What does 'shattered' mean here?
options: She was furious and immediately lodged an official appeal
She was devastated — deeply upset and broken by the news
She was too exhausted from training to feel anything at all
She was shocked but quickly recovered and accepted it
quiz.answer: She was devastated — deeply upset and broken by the news
quiz.explanation:
Shattered (figuratively) means utterly devastated — emotionally broken.
• shattered = deeply distressed; having one's hopes or emotions broken apart
• Mirrors the literal sense: something shattered breaks into many pieces
• Also British informal: 'I'm shattered' = I am exhausted

quiz.question: The gallery announced it had acquired a rare Rembrandt. What had it done?
options: It had been offered the painting as a temporary loan
It had obtained the painting and made it its property
It had authenticated the painting as a genuine Rembrandt
It had insured the painting for its full market value
quiz.answer: It had obtained the painting and made it its property
quiz.explanation:
Acquired means obtained and made one's own through effort, purchase, or other means.
• acquired = gained possession of; obtained
• From Latin: 'acquirere' — to seek in addition to
• 'An acquired taste' = something that takes time to appreciate

quiz.question: The patient's labored breathing alarmed the doctors. What were they hearing?
options: Breathing that was unusually fast and shallow
Breathing that was slow, difficult, and requiring great effort
Breathing that was regular but unusually loud
Breathing that stopped briefly and then restarted, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Breathing that was slow, difficult, and requiring great effort
quiz.explanation:
Labored means requiring obvious effort — slow, difficult, and strained.
• labored = showing signs of strain; done with difficulty
• 'Labored breathing' = visibly effortful; 'a labored joke' = heavy and lacking ease
• From 'labor' — work; labored = as if it costs great work to do

quiz.question: She was enamored of the Italian countryside and returned every summer. What does 'enamored' mean?
options: She was very familiar with it after many years of visiting
She was deeply charmed and captivated by it
She considered it the most beautiful place she had seen
She was planning to buy property there eventually
quiz.answer: She was deeply charmed and captivated by it
quiz.explanation:
Enamored means deeply charmed by or in love with something.
• enamored = filled with love or admiration; captivated
• From Old French: 'enamourer' — to inflame with love
• 'Enamored of' = in love with or deeply fond of something

quiz.question: The painting had been carefully restored before going on display. What had been done to it?
options: It had been cleaned, repaired, and returned to its original condition
It had been reframed and mounted for exhibition
It had been authenticated and insured for the first time
It had been photographed in high resolution for the catalogue, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: It had been cleaned, repaired, and returned to its original condition
quiz.explanation:
Restored means returned to its original condition after damage or decay.
• restored = brought back to its original state through repair or conservation
• From Latin: 'restaurare' — to rebuild
• 'Restore' a painting, 'restore' a building, 'restore' democracy — always a return to an original state

quiz.question: Despite being untutored, her painting showed remarkable natural talent. What does 'untutored' mean?
options: She had taught herself by copying from masters
She had received no formal training or instruction
She had abandoned her studies before completing them
She had resisted all advice from professional teachers
quiz.answer: She had received no formal training or instruction
quiz.explanation:
Untutored means not formally taught — without professional training.
• untutored = lacking formal instruction; self-developed
• 'Un-' (not) + 'tutored' (taught by a tutor)
• 'Untutored talent' = natural and raw, not shaped by training

quiz.question: The wedding was marred by a sudden argument between the families. What did the argument do to the wedding?
options: It cut the celebration short before it had properly begun
It damaged and spoiled what should have been a perfect occasion
It drew attention away from the couple and onto the families, as the evidence showed
It caused several guests to leave before the reception ended
quiz.answer: It damaged and spoiled what should have been a perfect occasion
quiz.explanation:
Marred means damaged or spoiled — made less good by something negative.
• marred = impaired; having its quality or enjoyment ruined by something
• From Old English: 'merran' — to hinder, waste, destroy
• 'Marred by controversy', 'marred by poor weather' — something good, damaged

quiz.question: After years of night shifts, he had become inured to sleep deprivation. What does 'inured' mean?
options: He had learned to manage his sleep more efficiently
He had grown accustomed and hardened to its effects
He had developed medical symptoms from lack of sleep
He no longer noticed when he was tired
quiz.answer: He had grown accustomed and hardened to its effects
quiz.explanation:
Inured means accustomed to something unpleasant through long exposure — hardened to it.
• inured = made tough by repeated exposure; habituated to hardship
• From 'in use' — worked into normal use by habit
• 'Inured to pain', 'inured to criticism' — no longer affected by what once stung

quiz.question: The CEO gave a measured response to the crisis, neither panicking nor dismissing the concerns. What does 'measured' describe here?
options: The response had been planned well in advance
The response was carefully considered and controlled
The response addressed every concern raised by journalists
The response was longer than people expected
quiz.answer: The response was carefully considered and controlled
quiz.explanation:
Measured means carefully considered and restrained — neither too much nor too little.
• measured = deliberate and restrained; not impulsive
• 'Measured response', 'measured tone' — controlled and calibrated
• Implies thought and judgment, as opposed to a knee-jerk reaction

quiz.question: The young conductor handled the difficult symphony with an assured performance. What does 'assured' describe?
options: A performance that was faithful to the composer's intentions, as the evidence showed
A performance given with confident competence and no trace of doubt
A performance that had been very thoroughly rehearsed
A performance widely praised by the critics in attendance
quiz.answer: A performance given with confident competence and no trace of doubt
quiz.explanation:
Assured means confident, competent, and settled — free from doubt or hesitation.
• assured = confident; having or showing certainty in one's abilities
• From Latin: 'assecurare' — to make safe, certain
• 'An assured performance' = calm authority; 'assured of success' = certain of it

quiz.question: When the equipment failed, the surgeon improvised using whatever was available. What did she do?
options: She waited until the correct equipment could be found
She made do by creating solutions on the spot without preparation
She abandoned the procedure and rescheduled for the following day, as the evidence showed
She requested emergency support from another department
quiz.answer: She made do by creating solutions on the spot without preparation
quiz.explanation:
Improvised means made or done without preparation — invented on the spot.
• improvised = created without prior preparation; made up as you go
• From Latin: 'improvisus' — unforeseen
• 'Improvised solution', 'improvised music' — spontaneous, unplanned

quiz.question: Despite the shocking news, she remained perfectly composed. What does 'composed' describe here?
options: She had prepared herself mentally for the news beforehand
She was calm, controlled, and not visibly disturbed
She chose not to react until she was alone
She was numb and not yet processing what had happened
quiz.answer: She was calm, controlled, and not visibly disturbed
quiz.explanation:
Composed means calm and in control of one's emotions — unruffled.
• composed = having one's emotions in order; serene and controlled
• 'Composure' is the noun — 'keeping one's composure' = staying calm
• Distinct from 'unfeeling' — a composed person feels but does not lose control

quiz.question: After the difficult meeting, he was not disposed to hear any more complaints. What does 'disposed' mean here?
options: He was physically unable to stay in the room any longer
He was not inclined or willing to do that thing
He had already made a formal decision on the matter
He was in a hurry to leave for another appointment
quiz.answer: He was not inclined or willing to do that thing
quiz.explanation:
Disposed (in this context) means inclined — tending toward a particular attitude or action.
• disposed = having a tendency or inclination (to do something)
• 'Well-disposed toward' = favorably inclined; 'not disposed to' = unwilling
• From Latin: 'disponere' — to arrange; one's mind is 'arranged' toward something

quiz.question: Sales figures were depressed throughout the winter quarter. What does 'depressed' mean here?
options: The figures had been incorrectly recorded due to a system error, as the evidence showed
The figures were lower than normal, pushed down by poor conditions
The figures showed a sudden and dramatic one-off drop
The figures were being investigated for possible fraud
quiz.answer: The figures were lower than normal, pushed down by poor conditions
quiz.explanation:
Depressed (in this context) means reduced below normal — suppressed or pushed down.
• depressed = reduced; lower than it should be
• From Latin: 'deprimere' — to press down
• 'A depressed economy', 'depressed wages' — pushed to a lower level

quiz.question: The psychologist said the patient's anger had been repressed for decades. What does 'repressed' mean?
options: The anger had been expressed in unhealthy ways over the years, as the evidence showed
The anger had been suppressed and kept from conscious awareness
The anger had faded naturally as the patient aged
The anger had been directed inward, causing self-harm
quiz.answer: The anger had been suppressed and kept from conscious awareness
quiz.explanation:
Repressed means held down and excluded from conscious thought — forced into the unconscious.
• repressed = (of feelings) suppressed; kept from conscious expression by psychological defense
• From Latin: 'reprimere' — to press back
• A key concept in Freudian psychology: repressed emotions resurface in other ways

quiz.question: The interviewer was clearly impressed by the candidate's preparation. What does 'impressed' mean here?
options: She had noted the candidate's preparation in writing
She had formed a strongly favorable opinion of the candidate
She had decided to offer the candidate the position
She was surprised the candidate had prepared so thoroughly, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: She had formed a strongly favorable opinion of the candidate
quiz.explanation:
Impressed means having had a strong, favorable effect on someone's opinion.
• impressed = affected with admiration; struck favorably
• From Latin: 'imprimere' — to press into (a positive mark has been made)
• 'I was impressed' = something made a strong, positive stamp on my judgment

quiz.question: The musician played as if possessed — wild-eyed, completely lost in the music. What does 'possessed' suggest here?
options: She was technically playing far beyond her normal ability
She appeared to be driven by a force beyond her normal self
She was so tired she was no longer thinking about the notes, as the evidence showed
She was playing from memory without looking at the score
quiz.answer: She appeared to be driven by a force beyond her normal self
quiz.explanation:
Possessed (figuratively) means appearing to be under the control of a powerful external force.
• possessed = driven as if by an external power; acting with abnormal intensity
• From Latin: 'possidere' — to occupy, take hold of
• 'Like a man possessed' = with frenzied, uncontrollable energy

quiz.question: Romeo and Juliet are the most famous star-crossed lovers in literature. What does 'star-crossed' mean?
options: They were famous and celebrated across the world
Their fate was thwarted by bad luck and opposing forces
They loved each other passionately but unpredictably, as the evidence showed
Their story ended ambiguously, without resolution
quiz.answer: Their fate was thwarted by bad luck and opposing forces
quiz.explanation:
Star-crossed means thwarted by fate — doomed to fail by opposing forces.
• star-crossed = destined to failure; ill-fated
• From astrology: the stars are 'crossed' against the couple, opposing their love
• Shakespeare coined the term in the prologue to Romeo and Juliet

quiz.question: The investor used a sophisticated strategy involving derivatives and hedging. What does 'sophisticated' mean here?
options: The strategy was expensive and required a large capital base
The strategy was complex and required advanced knowledge
The strategy had been proven successful over many decades
The strategy was innovative and untested in real markets
quiz.answer: The strategy was complex and required advanced knowledge
quiz.explanation:
Sophisticated means having a complex, subtle understanding — developed beyond the basic.
• sophisticated = highly developed; showing refined and complex knowledge
• From Greek: 'sophistes' — expert
• 'Sophisticated argument', 'sophisticated technology' — complex and refined

quiz.question: The dilapidated farmhouse had not been lived in for thirty years. What did it look like?
options: Old but structurally sound and recently cleaned
In a state of disrepair and falling apart from neglect
Abandoned but preserved by the dry climate
Crumbling in places but still habitable with renovation
quiz.answer: In a state of disrepair and falling apart from neglect
quiz.explanation:
Dilapidated means in a state of decay and disrepair through neglect.
• dilapidated = ruined; fallen into disrepair
• From Latin: 'dilapidare' — to scatter like stones (to-de-stone, dismantle)
• 'Dilapidated building' = crumbling, neglected, far gone in decay

quiz.question: The project was an unmitigated disaster. What does 'unmitigated' mean here?
options: The disaster could have been much worse than it was
The disaster was complete — without any redeeming aspect
The disaster was caused by a single avoidable decision
The disaster resulted in financial but not reputational damage
quiz.answer: The disaster was complete — without any redeeming aspect
quiz.explanation:
Unmitigated means absolute — not lessened or moderated in any way.
• unmitigated = not reduced or made less severe; complete and utter
• From Latin: 'mitigare' — to soften
• 'An unmitigated disaster' = 100% bad, with nothing to soften it

quiz.question: The geologist examined the striated rock surface left by the retreating glacier. What did she see?
options: A smooth, polished surface shaped by water erosion
A surface marked with parallel grooves and scratches
A rough, pitted surface from freeze-thaw weathering, as the evidence showed
A fractured surface from tectonic pressure
quiz.answer: A surface marked with parallel grooves and scratches
quiz.explanation:
Striated means marked with striae — parallel grooves or lines.
• striated = having stripes or narrow grooves running in parallel
• From Latin: 'stria' — a groove or furrow
• Glacial striations are scratches carved into bedrock by moving ice

quiz.question: After the enormous feast, the guests felt satiated. What does 'satiated' mean?
options: Tired from eating too much and needing to rest
Fully satisfied — having had more than enough
Slightly uncomfortable but glad they had indulged
Grateful to the host for such generous portions
quiz.answer: Fully satisfied — having had more than enough
quiz.explanation:
Satiated means having one's appetite fully satisfied — to the point of excess.
• satiated = having had one's fill completely
• From Latin: 'satiare' — to fill to the full
• Stronger than 'satisfied' — satiated implies almost too much

quiz.question: To the uninitiated, the chess board looked like a random arrangement of pieces. What does 'uninitiated' mean?
options: Extremely young or inexperienced in life generally
Unfamiliar with the rules or practices of a particular activity
Not yet officially recognized as a member of the group, as the evidence showed
Too intimidated to ask for an explanation
quiz.answer: Unfamiliar with the rules or practices of a particular activity
quiz.explanation:
The uninitiated are people with no knowledge or experience of a particular skill or practice.
• uninitiated = having no knowledge of a specialized subject or activity
• From Latin: 'initiare' — to begin, admit (as into a secret or practice)
• 'To the uninitiated' = for those who haven't learned the ropes yet

quiz.question: The newspaper printed unsubstantiated claims about the celebrity and later issued an apology. What does 'unsubstantiated' mean?
options: The claims had been denied by the celebrity's lawyers
The claims were not backed by sufficient evidence
The claims were technically true but presented misleadingly
The claims were so serious they required legal verification
quiz.answer: The claims were not backed by sufficient evidence
quiz.explanation:
Unsubstantiated means not supported by enough evidence — not proven.
• unsubstantiated = lacking proof or supporting evidence
• 'Un-' (not) + 'substantiated' (proven with substance/evidence)
• 'Unsubstantiated rumor', 'unsubstantiated allegation' — asserted without proof

quiz.question: He sent a belated birthday card a week after her birthday. What does 'belated' mean?
options: It was hand-delivered rather than posted
It arrived or was done later than it should have been
It was more elaborate than a standard birthday card, as the evidence showed
It came with an apology for missing the celebration
quiz.answer: It arrived or was done later than it should have been
quiz.explanation:
Belated means coming later than expected — overdue.
• belated = delayed beyond the proper or expected time
• From 'be-' + 'lated' (from 'late')
• 'Belated apology', 'belated recognition' — better late than never, but late nonetheless

quiz.question: When the prize was given to someone else, he looked completely deflated. What does 'deflated' mean here?
options: He was angry and refused to congratulate the winner, as the evidence showed
He lost all confidence and enthusiasm, as if the air had gone out of him
He was embarrassed to have expected to win
He was too tired from the competition to respond
quiz.answer: He lost all confidence and enthusiasm, as if the air had gone out of him
quiz.explanation:
Deflated (figuratively) means suddenly losing confidence, pride, or enthusiasm.
• deflated = reduced in self-confidence or spirit; let down
• Mirrors the literal sense: a balloon deflates when the air goes out
• 'Feeling deflated' = flat, dispirited, enthusiasm gone

quiz.question: The sudden change of plan left the tourists completely discombobulated. What does 'discombobulated' mean?
options: Angry at the lack of communication from the tour operator
Confused and disoriented, not sure what to do
Disappointed but resigned to the new arrangements
Exhausted after the previous day's activities
quiz.answer: Confused and disoriented, not sure what to do
quiz.explanation:
Discombobulated means confused and disconcerted — thrown off balance.
• discombobulated = flustered; in a state of disoriented confusion
• A humorous American coinage, possibly from 'discompose' + 'bamboozle'
• 'Discombobulated' sounds as scrambled as it feels

quiz.question: The museum catalogue described the ancient manuscript as maculated from centuries of exposure. What did it mean?
options: The manuscript had been repaired with pieces from another document
The manuscript was spotted and stained with marks of age
The manuscript was missing several pages from its original binding
The manuscript had been damaged by insects eating the parchment
quiz.answer: The manuscript was spotted and stained with marks of age
quiz.explanation:
Maculated means spotted or stained — marked with spots or blemishes.
• maculated = marked with spots, blotches, or stains
• From Latin: 'macula' — spot, blemish
• Related: 'immaculate' = spotless (without a single macula)

quiz.question: His calculated insult — delivered with a smile — was far more hurtful than an outburst would have been. What does 'calculated' mean here?
options: The insult was so extreme it caused a physical reaction
The insult was deliberately planned to achieve a specific effect
The insult was disguised so cleverly that few noticed it
The insult reflected genuine feelings he normally suppressed, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: The insult was deliberately planned to achieve a specific effect
quiz.explanation:
Calculated means deliberately planned or intended — done with cold forethought.
• calculated = done with full awareness of the likely effect; coldly deliberate
• From 'calculate' — to compute; everything has been worked out in advance
• 'Calculated risk', 'calculated insult' — not accidental or impulsive

quiz.question: The report warned that the unregulated market was open to exploitation and fraud. What does 'unregulated' mean?
options: The market was too small to attract official scrutiny
The market operated without official rules or oversight
The market was illegal but tolerated by the authorities
The market was new and regulation had not yet been proposed
quiz.answer: The market operated without official rules or oversight
quiz.explanation:
Unregulated means not controlled by rules or official oversight.
• unregulated = not subject to regulation; free from legal controls
• 'Un-' (not) + 'regulated' (controlled by rules)
• 'Unregulated industry' = operating without checks or accountability

quiz.question: The dinner table fell silent when she left, but had been animated before her departure. What does 'animated' mean here?
options: Everyone had been polite and formally well-behaved
The conversation had been lively and full of energy
People had been arguing loudly and interrupting each other
The evening had been carefully planned to be entertaining
quiz.answer: The conversation had been lively and full of energy
quiz.explanation:
Animated means lively and energetic — full of spirit and movement.
• animated = full of life and energy; vivid and enthusiastic
• From Latin: 'animare' — to fill with breath or life
• 'Animated discussion', 'animated expression' — alive with energy

quiz.question: The opposition team had badly underestimated their rivals. What had they done wrong?
options: They had not scouted the rivals' recent matches
They had judged the rivals to be less capable than they actually were
They had underinvested in their own preparation
They had relied on outdated statistics for their analysis, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: They had judged the rivals to be less capable than they actually were
quiz.explanation:
Underestimated means assessed as less capable, large, or significant than is actually the case.
• underestimated = rated too low; not given sufficient credit
• 'Under-' (too low) + 'estimated' (judged)
• 'Never underestimate your opponent' — failure to do so is a tactical error

quiz.question: The opinionated guest dominated the talk show, refusing to let others speak. What does 'opinionated' mean?
options: Highly knowledgeable and well-qualified to comment
Stubbornly assertive of one's own views, with little time for others
Passionate and persuasive in how they argue their case
Provocative and deliberately saying things to cause controversy, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Stubbornly assertive of one's own views, with little time for others
quiz.explanation:
Opinionated means strongly and stubbornly holding one's own opinions — dogmatically assertive.
• opinionated = conceitedly assertive; expressing strong views and dismissing others
• 'Opinionated' carries a negative tone — beyond confidence into stubbornness
• Distinct from 'principled' — opinionated implies inflexibility, not integrity

quiz.question: The report described the company's bloated bureaucracy. What was the problem?
options: The bureaucracy was secretive and hard to hold to account
The bureaucracy was excessively large and inefficient
The bureaucracy was resistant to any kind of change
The bureaucracy was too slow due to outdated technology
quiz.answer: The bureaucracy was excessively large and inefficient
quiz.explanation:
Bloated means swollen beyond healthy or appropriate size — inflated and inefficient.
• bloated = excessively enlarged; swollen and distended
• Literally: a bloated stomach; figuratively: a bloated organization, budget
• Implies unhealthy excess — too much, all in the wrong places

quiz.question: The celebrated author was awarded an honorary degree. What does 'celebrated' tell us about her?
options: She had recently published her most successful book
She was famous and widely praised in her field
She was a favorite among literary prize committees
She had received other honorary degrees previously
quiz.answer: She was famous and widely praised in her field
quiz.explanation:
Celebrated means famous and praised by many — widely admired.
• celebrated = renowned; widely known and admired
• From Latin: 'celebrare' — to frequent, honor
• 'A celebrated writer' = both famous and praised, not merely well-known

quiz.question: She used a serrated knife to cut the bread. What was distinctive about the blade?
options: It was unusually long and narrow for precision cutting
It had a jagged edge with regular tooth-like notches
It was very thin and flexible for slicing delicate foods
It was weighted at the tip for better downward pressure
quiz.answer: It had a jagged edge with regular tooth-like notches
quiz.explanation:
Serrated means having a jagged edge like the teeth of a saw.
• serrated = having saw-like notches along the edge
• From Latin: 'serra' — a saw
• 'Serrated blade', 'serrated leaves' — the edge is not smooth but notched

quiz.question: The soil was saturated after three days of rain. What does 'saturated' mean?
options: The soil was too compact to absorb any further water
The soil was completely soaked and could hold no more water
The soil had been eroded by the force of the rainfall, as the evidence showed
The soil had become too acidic for most crops
quiz.answer: The soil was completely soaked and could hold no more water
quiz.explanation:
Saturated means holding the maximum amount of something — unable to absorb any more.
• saturated = soaked to capacity; holding as much as possible
• From Latin: 'saturare' — to fill to capacity
• Also chemistry: 'a saturated solution' can dissolve no more of the substance

quiz.question: She was sated after just one slice of the rich chocolate cake. What does 'sated' mean?
options: She had been surprised by how sweet the cake was
She had eaten or experienced enough to be fully satisfied
She found the cake too heavy after the large main course, as the evidence showed
She wanted more but knew she should not have it
quiz.answer: She had eaten or experienced enough to be fully satisfied
quiz.explanation:
Sated means having had enough — fully satisfied, even to excess.
• sated = fully satisfied; having had one's fill completely
• From Latin: 'satis' — enough
• Slightly stronger than satisfied — sated implies the appetite is completely fulfilled

quiz.question: The prosecutor argued the crime was premeditated. Why does this matter legally?
options: It meant the criminal had a history of similar offenses
It meant the crime was planned in advance, not committed impulsively
It meant the criminal had acted alone without any outside help, as the evidence showed
It meant the crime was motivated by financial gain
quiz.answer: It meant the crime was planned in advance, not committed impulsively
quiz.explanation:
Premeditated means planned before it is carried out — deliberate and thought through in advance.
• premeditated = planned beforehand; not spontaneous
• From Latin: 'prae-' (before) + 'meditare' (to think over)
• 'Premeditated murder' carries a heavier charge than a crime of passion

quiz.question: The defense argued the attack was unpremeditated. What was the lawyer claiming?
options: The client had no prior criminal record
The client acted without prior planning — on impulse
The client had not intended to cause serious injury
The client was under the influence of medication at the time
quiz.answer: The client acted without prior planning — on impulse
quiz.explanation:
Unpremeditated means not planned in advance — done on the spur of the moment.
• unpremeditated = spontaneous; not thought out beforehand
• Key legal distinction: unpremeditated crimes are treated as less culpable
• Direct opposite of premeditated

quiz.question: The witness was visibly agitated as she gave her account. What does 'agitated' describe?
options: She was speaking too quickly for the court stenographer
She was restless, anxious, and unable to remain calm
She was holding back important details from her testimony
She was upset at being questioned so directly
quiz.answer: She was restless, anxious, and unable to remain calm
quiz.explanation:
Agitated means in a state of anxiety and restlessness — disturbed and unsettled.
• agitated = troubled and nervous; unable to settle or be at ease
• From Latin: 'agitare' — to drive, stir up
• 'Agitated' implies visible physical signs: trembling, fidgeting, rapid speech

quiz.question: The designer's understated approach used simple lines and a muted palette. What does 'understated' describe?
options: A style that was deliberately retro and nostalgic
A restrained style that avoids drawing attention to itself
A minimal style that sacrificed comfort for appearance
A neutral style intended to appeal to the widest possible audience
quiz.answer: A restrained style that avoids drawing attention to itself
quiz.explanation:
Understated means subtle and restrained — deliberately not showy or exaggerated.
• understated = not drawing attention to itself; expressing less than the full force
• 'Understated elegance' = refined, quiet, confident
• Opposite of bombastic or ostentatious — the power lies in restraint

quiz.question: The pain relief was administered in graduated doses over twelve hours. What does 'graduated' mean here?
options: The doses had been tested and approved through clinical trials
The doses increased in small, measured increments
The doses were varied depending on the patient's weight
The doses were given at irregular intervals as needed
quiz.answer: The doses increased in small, measured increments
quiz.explanation:
Graduated means arranged in a series of regular, graded steps.
• graduated = marked by gradual steps or degrees; increasing incrementally
• From Latin: 'gradus' — a step
• 'Graduated scale', 'graduated response' — moving step by step

quiz.question: The technology department was running on superannuated equipment that struggled to keep up with demand. What does 'superannuated' mean?
options: Expensive equipment that was difficult to justify on the budget
Out-of-date equipment that was too old to function effectively
Borrowed equipment that needed to be returned to its owner
Upgraded equipment that had not been properly tested
quiz.answer: Out-of-date equipment that was too old to function effectively
quiz.explanation:
Superannuated means obsolete or too old for current use — retired through age.
• superannuated = rendered obsolete by age; outdated
• From Latin: 'super annum' — beyond the year (i.e., past its prime)
• Also: a superannuated employee is one retired on a pension

quiz.question: The company's antiquated filing system relied entirely on paper records. What does 'antiquated' mean?
options: The system was complex and required specialist knowledge
The system was old-fashioned and unsuited to modern needs
The system was secure because it was not connected to the internet
The system had been inherited from a previous owner of the business
quiz.answer: The system was old-fashioned and unsuited to modern needs
quiz.explanation:
Antiquated means so old as to be out of date and no longer suitable.
• antiquated = old-fashioned and obsolete; no longer fit for purpose
• From Latin: 'antiquare' — to make old
• Stronger than 'old' — antiquated implies it is out of step with modern requirements

quiz.question: He was infatuated with his new neighbor — he rearranged his entire schedule just to walk past her house. What does 'infatuated' mean?
options: He was deeply and maturely in love with her
He was struck by an intense but irrational attraction or obsession
He was too shy to approach her directly
He admired her from a distance without any real feelings, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: He was struck by an intense but irrational attraction or obsession
quiz.explanation:
Infatuated means possessed by an intense but foolish and irrational attraction.
• infatuated = overwhelmed by foolish passion; besotted
• From Latin: 'infatuare' — to make foolish
• Stronger than a 'crush' — infatuation clouds judgment and may be all-consuming

quiz.question: She had a cultivated taste in music, preferring Baroque counterpoint to popular chart songs. What does 'cultivated' mean here?
options: Her taste in music was unusual and hard to explain
Her taste was refined through education and deliberate exposure
Her taste was expensive and difficult to indulge on a budget
Her taste was influenced by her upbringing and family background
quiz.answer: Her taste was refined through education and deliberate exposure
quiz.explanation:
Cultivated means refined and well-developed through education and care — like a cultivated garden.
• cultivated = developed through education and deliberate effort; refined
• From Latin: 'cultivare' — to till, tend, improve
• 'A cultivated person' has worked to develop taste, knowledge, and manner

quiz.question: She said she was forever indebted to the mentor who had helped her career. What does 'indebted' mean here?
options: She owed him money she had not yet repaid
She felt a deep gratitude and sense of obligation toward him
She had agreed to help him with his work in return
She credited him publicly in all her professional work, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: She felt a deep gratitude and sense of obligation toward him
quiz.explanation:
Indebted means owing gratitude or a sense of obligation — not necessarily financially.
• indebted = feeling grateful and obliged to someone for help given
• From 'in debt' — but the debt is moral or emotional, not necessarily monetary
• 'I am indebted to you' = I owe you a great deal; thank you deeply

quiz.question: The student was too distracted to study — his phone kept buzzing. What does 'distracted' mean?
options: He was bored and could not find motivation to begin
His attention was pulled away from what he should have been doing
He was tired and kept losing the thread of what he was reading
He was worried about an upcoming exam and could not concentrate, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: His attention was pulled away from what he should have been doing
quiz.explanation:
Distracted means having one's attention drawn away — unable to focus.
• distracted = having concentration broken by competing demands
• From Latin: 'distrahere' — to pull apart, pull in different directions
• 'Distracted driving', 'distracted student' — attention is elsewhere

quiz.question: After losing the match, the team sat dejected in the changing room. What does 'dejected' mean?
options: Exhausted after the physical demands of the game
Downcast and dispirited — low in mood and confidence
Angry at the referee's decisions during the match
Embarrassed by their performance in front of the home crowd
quiz.answer: Downcast and dispirited — low in mood and confidence
quiz.explanation:
Dejected means low in spirits — sad and discouraged.
• dejected = downhearted; feeling low after disappointment
• From Latin: 'deicere' — to cast down
• 'A dejected look', 'dejected silence' — slumped, quiet, without hope

quiz.question: Despite the emergency, the nurse remained collected throughout. What does 'collected' describe here?
options: She had gathered all the necessary equipment in advance
She was calm, composed, and in full control of herself
She was experienced enough to work without any assistance
She remained professional even when colleagues around her panicked
quiz.answer: She was calm, composed, and in full control of herself
quiz.explanation:
Collected means calm and in command of one's emotions — self-possessed.
• collected = composed; having gathered one's thoughts and emotions together
• 'Collected' implies the pieces of oneself have been brought together — no part is scattered
• 'Cool, calm, and collected' — the classic triad of composure under pressure
